extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters initiated by versus against the Bank, directors and promoters. I counted only the “Against the Bank” row: criminal proceedings Nil (0), tax proceedings 1, statutory/regulatory proceedings Nil (0), and excluded the Bank’s “By the Bank” row, including 207 criminal proceedings and ₹633.89 million, as well as all “By” rows; no matters against directors or promoters were counted. The quantified group amount against the Bank is the explicitly disclosed ₹12.21 million relating to the direct tax dispute, which is also the single material civil matter and is not double-counted; this converts from ₹12.21 million to ₹1.221 crore. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated Total of ₹529.18 million as at September 30, 2023, converted to ₹52.918 crore, rather than a sum of line items. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer revenue concentration percentage is disclosed; the revenue percentages shown relate to interest earned or other business metrics, not customer concentration. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
